title: Fixing CSS Pseudo-Element Styles with Inconsistent Box Model

statement.markdown: Given a CSS stylesheet, fix the bug in the pseudo-element styles to ensure consistent box model behavior across browsers. The stylesheet has multiple rules with before and after pseudo-elements, and some of them have inconsistent box-sizing properties. The task is to update the box-sizing properties to ensure that the pseudo-elements behave correctly and have the expected dimensions.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the CSS stylesheet with the buggy pseudo-element styles.
statement.outputFormat: The corrected CSS stylesheet with consistent box model behavior.
statement.notes: Time and space complexity are not a concern in this task.

constraints.description: Number of CSS rules with pseudo-elements

examples.explanation: Updated box-sizing property for the after pseudo-element to ensure consistent box model behavior.
Updated box-sizing property for the before pseudo-element to ensure consistent box model behavior.

hints.text: Check the box-sizing properties of the pseudo-elements.

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Pseudo-Element Styles
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this task is to understand the box model and how it applies to pseudo-elements. The goal is to ensure that the pseudo-elements have the expected dimensions and behave consistently across browsers.
editorial.bruteForce: One approach to solving this task is to manually update the box-sizing properties for each pseudo-element. However, this can be time-consuming and prone to errors.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach is to use a CSS preprocessor like Sass or Less to automatically update the box-sizing properties.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a CSS framework that takes care of the box model and pseudo-element styles for you.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected stylesheet should have consistent box model behavior across browsers.
